runs.llm_decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C) by ~4.26°C. Previous attempts to maximize spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500 W/mK), and geometry changes are constrained by legality limits. The most effective remaining lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve heat extraction from the spreader to the side sinks. We will increase base_material conductivity by 1.5x (from 12.0 to 18.0), which is within the allowed [0.5x, 2.0x] step ratio and bounds [5.0, 250.0].

The current design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 68.85°C, well below the 85.0°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is the primary lever for improving lateral heat spreading and reducing peak temperatures.

Increasing both width and height will expand the thermal cross-section and lateral spreading area, offering a new path to reduce the chip peak temperature.
